# Movemental promo — “Two equal errors” → AI Stewardship Sequence (full script + storyboard)

**Working title:** *Fragmentation, not merely tools* (alt: *Not a tools problem*)  
**Runtime target:** ~2:40–3:00 voice-over after this pass (re-time on read); cutdowns at 0:60 and 0:30 noted below  
**Primary audience:** Senior leaders in mission-driven orgs (pastor, ED, founder, institutional lead)  
**Canon sources:** [`docs/articles/the-movemental-thesis.md`](../../articles/the-movemental-thesis.md), [`docs/articles/the-two-equal-errors.md`](../../articles/the-two-equal-errors.md), [`docs/articles/the-fragmentation-tax.md`](../../articles/the-fragmentation-tax.md), [`docs/articles/this-is-not-a-tools-problem.md`](../../articles/this-is-not-a-tools-problem.md), [`docs/articles/the-ssss-framework.md`](../../articles/the-ssss-framework.md); on-site spine in `home-concept-modern-page-content.tsx` (two errors + stakes + path).

**Voice:** Calm, direct, editorial — no panic, no vendor hype. Plain sentences; one memorable line per beat. Always introduce the full phrase — **the AI Stewardship Sequence: Safety, Sandbox, Skills, Solutions** — on first mention before using any shorter reference (per [`docs/content/terminology-registers.md`](../../content/terminology-registers.md)).

**Visual system (production):** Light editorial default; optional **Midnight** band for the “path” act — match [`docs/design/DESIGN.md`](../../design/DESIGN.md) (tonal stacking, Inter, primary for CTAs only, no decorative borders for sectioning). Prefer **concrete organizational artifacts** (board packet, teaching outline, donor draft, governance doc, workflow card) over generic “premium emptiness” B-roll.

---

## 1. Purpose & promise (for the team)

| Goal | Deliver |
| ---- | ------- |
| Reframe | AI is in the room; the deepest failure mode is **fragmentation** of informational and relational intelligence — not “wrong model.” |
| Diagnose | Two **equal** errors — sharpen **organizational consequences** (rush vs freeze), then refuse both flinches. |
| Forbid | Neither freeze nor hustle is leadership; the middle of two errors is still inside the mistake. |
| Bridge | **Why the AI Stewardship Sequence exists:** not posture alone — an **ordered path** that turns concern into capability without sacrificing integrity. |
| Offer | Safety → Sandbox → Skills → Solutions — **order is the framework**; **formation** named as distinctive, not buried. |
| Build | Movemental **helps build** governance, experiments, formation, **and** the systems / workflows / infrastructure / pathways that let it **hold**. |
| CTA | **Artifact-led:** field guide + framework + conversation (not generic “contact us”). |

---

## 2. On-screen supers (optional burn-in)

Use sparingly; let VO carry argument.

1. `The question is not whether AI will touch your organization.`  
2. `Two equal errors`  
3. `They produce the same failure` (alt to “same mistake / uniforms” — test on read)  
4. `A fragmentation problem` (pair once with fuller VO)  
5. `Informational · relational intelligence` (optional two-line card)  
6. `Safety → Sandbox → Skills → Solutions`  
7. `The order is the framework`  
8. `Read the field guide` / `movemental.com` (end card ladder)

---

## 3. Full voice-over script (master — pass 2)

**Approx. word count:** ~520 words @ measured pace — **re-time** in session; may land ~2:45–3:00.

---

**[COLD OPEN — 0:00–0:18]**  
*Tone: quiet certainty. No music swell yet.*

If you hold senior responsibility in a mission-driven organization, you already feel it: the frontier is under your feet whether you chose it or not.

So here is the honest question — not whether artificial intelligence will touch your work. It already does.

The question is whether your response will be worthy of the mission you serve.

---

**[ACT I — TWO EQUAL ERRORS — 0:18–0:58]**  
*Music: subtle pulse; editorial restraint.*

When it comes to AI, there are **two equal errors**.

They look like opposites. **They produce the same failure.**

One error is to **head recklessly down a potentially unsafe path** — mandates, pilots that become production by accident, volume that arrives faster than judgment. That path can **outrun integrity** — trust frays, coherence frays, and you discover you are moving faster than your ethics can govern.

The other is to **stand still in an already unsafe place** — a blanket “no” that feels like stewardship but quietly trains your people to meet the technology everywhere except inside your values. That path does not buy you purity on paper. It invites **drift** — shadow adoption, half-decisions, and a slow **strategic irrelevance** the mission cannot afford.

Most organizations oscillate between the two.

**One path outruns judgment. The other abandons the field.**

Both skip the slow work only leadership can do: deciding **what kind of adoption** keeps you coherent with who you are and what you are for.

---

**[ACT II — FRAGMENTATION / TWO INTELLIGENCES — 0:58–1:38]**  
*Texture montage — paper, board edges, real artifacts; nothing “tech bro.”*

Here is what almost everyone gets wrong on the first pass.

We treat AI as a **tools problem** — procurement, platforms, training decks.

**This is not merely a tools problem. It is a fragmentation problem.**

**Artificial intelligence exposes and accelerates fragmentation** — in your organization’s **informational intelligence** and its **relational intelligence**. It touches **what you know** and **how you relate** — and whether the two still **cohere**.

That sequence — buy, deploy, train — worked for the last generation of software.

It fails here — not because the stack is uniquely mysterious, but because the pressure lands **upstream of tools** on a living organization.

**Who decides** what may be touched?  
**What are you forming** in the people who use it — craft, conscience, courage?

The deeper question is **not only** what the tool can do. It is **what its use is forming** in your people and your organization.

**What must remain human** — the conversations, the care, the words that should never sound interchangeable?

If the senior leader cannot state your organization’s relationship to AI in **one honest paragraph**, you do not yet have a relationship to AI.

You have **tool sprawl** — and the market is happy to keep selling you treads while you skip the stairs.

---

**[ACT III — WHAT YOU CANNOT DO — 1:38–1:58]**  
*Tighter cuts. Slight lift in rhythm.*

So what can’t you do?

You cannot **freeze** your way to safety. The terrain moves; your staff and donors already live in the new normal.

And you cannot **hustle** your way to wisdom. Speed without clarity does not rescue a broken workflow — it **accelerates** the break.

The middle of two errors is still inside the mistake.

---

**[ACT IV — WHY THE SEQUENCE / THE WAY FORWARD — 1:58–2:35]**  
*Music opens slightly; visuals shift to “path” / staircase metaphor.*

What organizations need is **not** a posture, **or** a pilot, **by itself**.

They need a **sequence** that can turn **concern** into **capability** without sacrificing **integrity**.

There is a structured way through — not a slogan, a **sequence** learned in public view:

**Safety** — governance with real authority, conviction-level honesty, plain-language boundaries on where AI does and does not belong.

**Sandbox** — bounded exploration with hypotheses, artifacts, and a learning loop — so the organization discovers what it is becoming **before** the mission pays the price.

**Skills** — **formation**, not mere training: discernment, authorship, stewardship — humans who can tell the difference between plausible output and **faithful** output.

**Solutions** — deployment into real workflows, last, on purpose — owned by people who can correct course, under policy that already functions.

Together, these four steps are the **AI Stewardship Sequence** — introduced only after the words are clear — because **the order is the framework.**  
Skip a step, and the later steps borrow trust from nothing solid.

---

**[CLOSE + CTA — 2:35–3:00]**  
*Music resolves; ladder on screen + VO.*

The only adoption that endures is adoption your organization can still **recognize as itself** a year later.

**Movemental** helps mission-driven organizations **navigate AI as one stewardship problem** — aligning governance, experimentation, formation, and deployment so the work remains **recognizable as itself**.

We do **not** only advise the sequence. We help organizations **build the conditions, workflows, and infrastructure** — including **pathways** where they serve the mission — that let trustworthy use **hold over time**.

That is not only a claim about models.

It is a claim about **integrity under pressure**.

**Read the field guide. See the framework. Start a conversation** — **movemental.com**.

---

## 4. Shot-by-shot storyboard (master — pass 2)

*Timecodes below are **editorial anchors** from the prior cut; re-confirm against VO read. Visuals skew toward **concrete mission org reality** (not generic premium emptiness).*

| T | Duration | Visual | Audio (VO) | Supers / type |
| - | -------- | ------ | ---------- | ------------- |
| 0:00 | 4s | **Board room / chapel / library** — real table, **agenda + name tent** edge in frame; empty chair; morning light — no sci-fi / robots | Cold open begins | — |
| 0:04 | 5s | Hands: **close laptop** → **open printed teaching outline** or sermon/workshop notes — human scale | “…whether you chose it or not.” | — |
| 0:09 | 4s | Leader mid-conversation; **fundraiser draft or donor thank-you** visible at edge of frame (blur OK) | “…whether your response…” | — |
| 0:13 | 5s | Hold on **listening** face; optional logo bug | “…worthy of the mission you serve.” | Logo bug optional |
| 0:18 | 3s | Split: **motion blur calendar** vs **frozen inbox + untouched policy PDF** | Act I headline | `Two equal errors` |
| 0:21 | 6s | **01** Reckless: stacked calendar invites + **“pilot → prod” sticky**; abstract notification noise | Rush error + **integrity / trust** consequence | `01` |
| 0:27 | 6s | **02** Freeze: **governance doc** in plastic sleeve, **“Q3 review”** sticky yellowed; **shadow** phone edge (implied personal tool use) | Freeze error + **drift / shadow adoption** | `02` |
| 0:33 | 5s | Subtle **pendulum** or same leader in **two meeting rooms** (no partisan cues) | “Oscillate…” + **one path / other path** contrast line | — |
| 0:38 | 8s | **Whiteboard** erased too fast → **pause** on blank board; marker cap audible | “Both skip the slow work…” | — |
| 0:46 | 6s | Doc: **“Our relationship to AI is…”** cursor blink; or **board packet** margin | “…coherent with who you are…” | — |
| 0:52 | 3s | **Texture**: linen + **printed agenda fiber** | Act II bridge | — |
| 0:55 | 6s | **Split visual**: left = blurred generic “stack” tiles (no real logos); right = **simple diagram: knowledge vs relationships drifting apart** | Tools framing → **fragmentation** turn | `A fragmentation problem` |
| 1:01 | 7s | **Org chart** — exec highlighted; **approval routing card** on desk | “…upstream of tools” + **Who decides** | — |
| 1:08 | 7s | **Mentor + staff** at one screen; **AI draft vs printed voice** (highlighter comparing lines) | Formation + **who we become** | — |
| 1:15 | 6s | **Pastoral prep**: open bible / notes + **care list**; or donor meeting — backs, respectful distance | “What must remain human…” | — |
| 1:21 | 4s | Hallway: leader asked hard question — **thinks**; **faculty pathway map** rolled under arm (optional) | “…one honest paragraph…” | — |
| 1:25 | 3s | **Workflow wall** — cards multiplying; lines **tangle** into noise (sprawl) | “…tool sprawl…” | — |
| 1:28 | 4s | **Door stuck / frost** on glass — light; or “road closed” on **wrong** door | “You cannot freeze…” | — |
| 1:32 | 4s | Keyboard → **smeared** illegible stream | “…cannot hustle…” | — |
| 1:36 | 5s | Midnight: **integrity · coherence · reality** (typography — not “impact” as cheap opposite) | “Middle of two errors…” | — |
| 1:41 | 3s | **Real staircase** silhouette — no elevator icon | Bridge into sequence | — |
| 1:44 | 5s | **Safety**: signed **governance cover sheet** + margin notes in red | “Safety…” | `Safety` |
| 1:49 | 5s | **Sandbox**: **lab bench** sticky hypotheses + **artifact printouts** | “Sandbox…” | `Sandbox` |
| 1:54 | 5s | **Skills**: **peer circle** + **red pen** on printout; “sounds like us but isn’t” energy | “Skills…” / formation | `Skills` |
| 1:59 | 5s | **Solutions**: boring **workflow diagram** on wall — approvals as boxes | “Solutions…” | `Solutions` |
| 2:04 | 4s | Four words + **camera lands top step** | “…order is the framework.” | `The order is the framework` |
| 2:08 | 5s | Pull back: **org as network** of rooms/teams — not lone hero | “…recognize as itself…” | — |
| 2:13 | 4s | **Wordmark** + optional founder line to camera | “…integrity under pressure” + build line | Movemental |
| 2:17 | 5s+ | End card: **Read the field guide** (primary), **See the framework**, **Start a conversation**, URL, optional QR | CTA ladder VO | `movemental.com` |

## 5. Cutdown: 60-second version (VO skeleton)

- **0:00–0:12** Cold open: frontier + worthy response.  
- **0:12–0:30** Two errors + **same failure** + **one consequence line each** (integrity vs drift).  
- **0:30–0:48** Not merely tools → **fragmentation** + **informational / relational** (one sentence each).  
- **0:48–1:00** “An ordered path without the integrity trade” + the AI Stewardship Sequence (one word each: Safety · Sandbox · Skills · Solutions) + **order is the framework** + field guide CTA.

## 7. Music & SFX brief

- **Genre:** restrained ambient / modern classical piano + soft pulses; **no** trailer braams, no “corporate startup uke.”  
- **SFX:** paper, **board book** page, chair, room tone — **avoid** digital whooshes except **one** tasteful transition at Act IV.  
- **Mix:** VO +2–3 dB above music; sidechain light ducking on piano.

---

## 8. Legal / brand checklist

- [ ] Do not imply specific AI vendor partnerships unless true.  
- [ ] Client logos only with written permission.  
- [ ] “AI Stewardship Sequence” always paired at least once with the full four words (Safety, Sandbox, Skills, Solutions) on-screen or in VO.  
- [ ] Match **DESIGN.md** tokens in any UI mockups used as B-roll.  
- [ ] Accessibility: captioned; contrast on supers verified for Midnight band.

## 11. Pass 2 — editorial intent (archive of critique)

This revision intentionally addresses: (1) diagnosis specificity vs generic “tools” talk; (2) organizational stakes on the two errors; (3) bridge from problem to **necessity** of an ordered path; (4) **product / infrastructure** dimension; (5) **formation** as distinctive; (6) **informational + relational** intelligence; (7) sobriety on “uniforms” line — swapped default to “same failure”; (8) **artifact ladder** CTA; (9) **concrete** B-roll direction; (10) integrated close vs list-like stewardship sentence.
